question_text: A student compares the gravitational force on three different objects on Earth: a feather (0.005 kg), a mobile phone (0.2 kg), and a textbook (1.5 kg). Which statement best explains the difference in their weights?

options.label: A
options.text: The feather has less weight because it experiences weaker gravity than the other objects.

options.label: B
options.text: All objects experience the same gravitational field strength on Earth (10 N/kg), but they have different masses, so their weights are proportional to their masses.

options.label: C
options.text: The textbook is heavier because it is larger in size, which makes gravity act more strongly on it.

options.label: D
options.text: The mobile phone has the most weight because it is made of dense materials like metal and glass.

explanation: All objects on Earth experience the same gravitational field strength of 10 N/kg, regardless of their size or material. Since weight = mass × g, and g is constant, the weight of each object depends only on its mass. The feather (0.05 N), phone (2 N), and textbook (15 N) have different weights because they have different masses.
